title: Animals in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Animals in Evangelion             Quote Post by Ether » Fri Aug 07, 2009 9:55 pm This seems a very random topic, but I realized it hadn't been discussed yet as far as I could see. We have already learned that humans are angels born from Lilith and the other angels are born from Adam. There was never any official explanation as to why animals were there though. Were they made by Adam or Lilith for a purpose? Were they already there? I know this seems really odd a topic, but I figured it would be interesting to seem some other people's take on this.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Aug 07, 2009 10:18 pm The show's mythology does not provide any explanation for animals. However I find it curious that Pen-Pen is present in instrumentality in the TV ending. I think it's another case of them not thinking of a way of explaining animals. That or humans are actually alien, and life already existed on this planet when the black egg of Lilith crashed here. But that's kind of weird considering the current theories on evolution. In b4 creationism lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Armitage » Sat Aug 08, 2009 1:51 am I always found Pen Pen to be an interesting case . While the literal mythology concerning animals may be of a much different issue , I was thinking of the allegorical meaning behind Pen Pen . Pen Pen is a warm water penguin , something that happened after Second Impact basically hurripwned the natural habitat of Penguins which are found almost exclusively in Antarctica . Second impact was basically a complete annihilation for them . However , "warm water" penguin kind of denotes a type of evolution . Penguins are forever changed . There will no longer be penguins that simply go in cold water . (Maybe there are , but again I'm talking allegorically here) . So , for me , Pen Pen , is a sign of a major theme in Eva: Evolution through Crisis . The way Second Impact has affected Antarctica is a kind of Modernist twist on pen pen's brethren and only those who have adapted to living in the hot water of the times have lived . He's far from an existential character , however . Instead , he lives quite comfortably inside his high-tech refrigerator . In a sense , we can't say that he is completely Modern , but , through the elevation of his personhood (via instrumentality or via the very fact that he has a kind of sentience that while people may call comic , could also be interpreted as significant if we follow the levels of sentience e.g. bacteria - penpen - human - eva-samas) we can say that he is the paragon of the Post-Modern Penguin . Using technology to survive a world where God had abandoned him and almost not being troubled by it .

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Sat Aug 08, 2009 2:16 am I always thought that in the Eva universe, the Lillim started out as very basic lifeforms spawned from Lilith that evolved into a huge variety of lifeforms that all had potential to become intelligent, until one line reaches sentience as well as being capable of creating things and become the Lillim. I think that Lillim have to go through evolution to reach their final form, while Adam-based lifeforms appear at their peak form without having to go through a lengthly evolutionary process. All the animals would be sort of like proto-Lillim, creatures that never managed to evolve sentience or tool using comparable to our level before us. So animals could be thought of as failed bridges to becoming true Lillim.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 08, 2009 2:58 am The Eva Monkey wrote: That or humans are actually alien, and life already existed on this planet when the black egg of Lilith crashed here.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 08, 2009 12:09 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The show's mythology does not provide any explanation for animals.

[]:             Quote Post by Ether » Sat Aug 08, 2009 12:30 pm Nah. CI confirms that the other stuff is from Lilith, too.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 08, 2009 1:23 pm Reichu wrote: However I find it curious that Pen-Pen is present in instrumentality in the TV ending.
summary: []: Animals in Evangelion             Quote Post by Ether » Fri Aug 07, 2009 9:55 pm This seems a very random topic, but I realized it hadn't been discussed yet as far as I could see. We have already learned that humans are angels born from Lilith and the other angels are born from Adam. There was never any official explanation as to why animals were there though. Were they made by Adam or Lilith for a purpose? Were they already there?
